# logs on a job with no runs → exit 0, empty output (the job exists,
# it just hasn't fired yet — Unix-style empty-state, not an error).

exec eon add --cron '@hourly' --name quiet -- echo hi

exec eon logs quiet
! stdout .

# Unknown name → exit 3.
! exec eon logs nope

# Unknown 5-char ID → exit 3.
! exec eon logs abcde

# Bad shorthand → exit 2 (cobra).
! exec eon logs -1
